spans.html:
namejob in the weekjob at the weekendhappy or not?
Carol Harris
Silvio Rossi

spans.content: From Monday to Friday, Carol Harris is ‘Doctor Harris’. She’s a doctor at a hospital in New York City. ‘The job is good, but it’s difficult,’ she says. ‘But the weekend … the weekend is good!’ At the weekend she isn’t Doctor Harris, she’s a singer at a club. ‘I’m happy with two jobs,’ she says. ‘One job in the week and one at the weekend!’

Silvio Rossi is from Rome, Italy. ‘I’m a taxi driver here in New York. The job is good and the money is OK,’ he says. On Saturday and Sunday, Silvio is a waiter at a restaurant. Is he happy with two jobs? ‘Yes, I am. I’m very happy,’ he says, ‘and New York is a great city.’
present simple be: he, she, it
5A Underline the verb be in the sentences.
1 From Monday to Friday, Carol Harris is ‘Doctor Harris’.
2 She's a doctor at a hospital in New York City.
3 At the weekend she isn't Doctor Harris, she's a singer at a club.
5 Is he happy with two jobs?
1 For the positive (+), we use he/she
2 For the negative (-), we use he/she
C Learn and practise. Go to the Grammar Bank.
6A Complete the conversations with the sentences (a–c).
B: Kevin is a British name. Is he from the UK?
a No, it isn't. It's in South Korea.
b Yes, he's a good friend.
c No, he isn't. He's from Busan.

A:

1 William 4 Sharon 7 Beba  
2 Rachel 5 Ian 8 Lajan  
3 Chris 6 Simnit 9 Tom

B Work in pairs. Match the sentences with the correct photos (A–I).
1 In my bag I have my phone and my wallet.
2 I have a bottle of water. I have my purse, my keys.
3 I have my laptop and two books.

5 I have a bottle of water, a notebook and a banana.
6 I have a sandwich in my bag.

5A Make a list of five or more objects in your bag, purse or wallet.
B Read the Key phrases. Which phrases are true for you?
I have five/six things in my bag.
I don't have a bag, but in my pocket/purse/wallet I have ...
I have a phone, a wallet and my glasses.
I don't know the name in English.

Luis has a laptop, a phone and ...
C Tell the class about one object that you all have. We all have a photo.
write a lost and found post
7 A Match the posts (1 and 2) with the private messages (a and b).
a small black purse. It has a credit card and about €5. Lost in the High Street today.
Please PM me or phone me on 911 8455. Thank you!
a key ring with three keys. Found in Adastra Park this afternoon.
Hi Jim, you have my keys.
Hi, Evi, I have your purse. My phone number is ...
B Choose one object from your bag. Write a lost post about the object.

page 151 SOUNDS AND SPELLING syllables, stress and /ə/; /s/, /z/, /ɪz/ in plurals
is / has ‘e’. The top adjective is ‘good’ and the top noun is ‘time’. The top
have / has five vowels: a, e, i, o and u. Greek has seven vowels and the
Poland / Polish alphabet has nine vowels.
is / has many words from other languages. 'Zero' is from Arabic, 'guitar' is from Spanish and 'shampoo' is from the Indian language Hindi. 'Café' and 'art' are
is / isn’t ‘wun’, it’s ‘one’; ‘2’ isn’t ‘too’, it’s ‘two’; and ‘8’ is ‘eight’. Crazy!
2 Mexico City is in Mexico / Mexican.
4 London is in the UK / British.
5 Scarlett Johansson is the USA / American.
B Write the name of a famous man, a famous woman and a city.
C Work in pairs. Student A: Say a name or a city. Student B: Say the country and nationality.
B: Seoul is in South Korea. It's South Korean.
2A Complete the conversation with the correct form of be. Use contractions where possible.
B Work in pairs. Student A: Look at the table and choose a letter (A–P). Student B: Ask questions. Guess the letter.

page 151 SOUNDS AND SPELLING syllables, stress and /ə/; /s/, /z/, /ɪz/ in plurals
is / has ‘e’. The top adjective is ‘good’ and the top noun is ‘time’. The top
have / has five vowels: a, e, i, o and u. Greek has seven vowels and the
Poland / Polish alphabet has nine vowels.
is / has many words from other languages. 'Zero' is from Arabic, 'guitar' is from Spanish and 'shampoo' is from the Indian language Hindi. 'Café' and 'art' are
is / isn’t ‘wun’, it’s ‘one’; ‘2’ isn’t ‘too’, it’s ‘two’; and ‘8’ is ‘eight’. Crazy!
